info.name: Auth – Update Profile (PATCH /api/v1/auth/me/profile)
info.description:
E2E tests for PATCH /api/v1/auth/me/profile (requires JWT).

Folder execution order (orchestrated by make e2e-update-profile):
  1. setup             — register gmail_test_email, fetch verification OTP from Gmail, verify email, login
  2. happy-path        — both fields → 200 + GET /me confirms DB writes;
                         display_name only → 200 + avatar_url unchanged in /me;
                         avatar_url only → 200 + display_name unchanged in /me;
                         same values re-sent → 200 accepted (no-op is intentional per D-03)
  3. failures          — empty patch → 422; blank display_name → 422; too-long display_name → 422;
                         control-char display_name → 422; avatar_url no scheme → 422;
                         avatar_url ftp:// scheme → 422; avatar_url too long → 422;
                         avatar_url empty string → 422
  4. auth-failures     — missing Authorization header → 401; tampered token signature → 401
  5. validation        — body > 1 MiB → 413; malformed JSON → 422
  6. rate-limiting-prof — prof:ip: bucket (burst=10): 10 warmups → 11th → 429 (Retry-After: 6)

Rate-limiter is inside the JWTAuth middleware group, so the JWT token stored in
collection variables is needed for warmup requests. All folders run in ONE
newman invocation so the token is preserved. The rate-limiting folder prerequest
pins _xff to 127.0.0.1 so its requests consume a separate IP bucket.

All test users use @e2e.test emails (or gmail_test_email) so `make _e2e-db-clean` can wipe them.
Run `make _e2e-db-clean` and `make _e2e-kv-clean` before this collection.

Notes on what is NOT covered here and why:
  - Individual field-validation variants (every rune-count boundary, every control char code point,
    every URL-scheme variant beyond ftp://): covered by handler_test.go.
  - Audit row event type and metadata content: requires a DB read step;
    covered by store_test.go TestUpdateProfileTx_AuditRowEventAndMetadata.
  - context.WithoutCancel absence from UpdateProfile: covered by service_test.go.
  - QuerierProxy error injection (FailUpdateUserProfile, FailInsertAuditLog): covered by store_test.go.
  - Token-level auth guard (expired token, wrong JTI): covered by handler_test.go.

exec: // Increment the request sequence counter and derive a unique X-Forwarded-For IP
// for every request so non-RL folders never exhaust any rate-limit bucket.
// The rate-limiting folder overrides _xff to 127.0.0.1 via its own prerequest.

item.name: setup
item.description: Registers the gmail_test_email user, fetches the verification OTP from Gmail, verifies the email, and logs in. All subsequent folders depend on prof_access_token being set.

exec: console.error('[ERROR] gmail_test_email not set in environment — add it to environment.json.');
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for verification email to arrive in Gmail...');
console.log('[INFO] Done waiting. Querying Gmail API...');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired — refresh at https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching verification email found in Gmail.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode verification email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found in verification email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Verification OTP stored in prof_vfy_code:', match[1]);
console.error('[ERROR] prof_vfy_code is empty — Gmail fetch in register step did not complete.');
console.error('        Check: gmail_access_token is set, token not expired, --delay-request >= 8000.');
console.log('[INFO] Access token stored in prof_access_token.');

item.name: happy-path
item.description:
Full update-profile scenarios in deterministic order.
  1.  PATCH both fields → 200, GET /me confirms both columns written to DB
  2.  PATCH display_name only → 200, GET /me confirms avatar_url unchanged
  3.  PATCH avatar_url only → 200, GET /me confirms display_name unchanged
  4.  Re-send same values (no-op) → 200 accepted (D-03: no read-before-write, no-op detection intentionally omitted)

exec: // Both display_name and avatar_url provided. UpdateUserProfile uses COALESCE so
// both columns are updated in a single query. Confirms wiring from handler → service → store.
// Store the values we just set so subsequent /me assertions can reference them.

item.name: GET /me → 200 (confirms both fields written to DB)

exec: // GET /me returns the live user row. Confirming display_name and avatar_url match
// the values we just patched proves the DB write committed and both columns updated.
// Only display_name provided. The COALESCE query passes NULL for avatar_url,
// so avatar_url must remain unchanged in the DB.
// avatar_url must still be the value we set in the first PATCH
// Only avatar_url provided. display_name must remain unchanged in the DB.
// display_name must still be 'Display Only' from the previous display_name-only PATCH

item.name: PATCH /me/profile → 200 (same values re-sent — no-op accepted, D-03)

exec: // D-03: no read-before-write, no no-op detection. Sending the same values is
// accepted as a valid update and returns 200. The audit row is still written.
// This confirms the handler does not short-circuit on unchanged values.
pm.test('status is 200 (no-op accepted per D-03)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));

item.name: failures
item.description:
Validation and domain-error paths for PATCH /me/profile.
All requests use prof_access_token (set in setup). These cases confirm the handler
validation wiring end-to-end — individual boundary variants are covered by handler_test.go.

NOT covered here (see handler_test.go):
  - display_name exactly 100 runes → 200 (boundary accepted)
  - display_name 101 runes vs other multi-byte rune combos
  - avatar_url exactly 2048 bytes → 200 (boundary accepted)
  - every ASCII control char variant beyond \x01

exec: // Both display_name and avatar_url are omitted. validateAndNormalise returns
// display_name of only whitespace trims to empty → ErrDisplayNameEmpty → 422.
// Build a 101-rune display_name (all ASCII 'a' for simplicity).
// validateDisplayName counts Unicode rune length, not byte length.

item.name: display_name contains ASCII control char → 422 validation_error

exec: // 'not-a-url' has no scheme → url.Parse returns no Host → ErrAvatarURLInvalid → 422.
// Only http:// and https:// are accepted schemes. ftp:// → ErrAvatarURLInvalid → 422.
// Build a valid https:// URL whose total byte length is 2049.
// 'https://x.com/' is 15 bytes; pad the path with 2049-15=2034 'a' chars.

item.name: avatar_url empty string → 422 validation_error (D-04: clearing is not supported)

exec: // D-04: explicit empty string is a validation error — clearing avatar_url is out of
// scope. validateAvatarURL rejects empty strings → ErrAvatarURLInvalid → 422.

item.name: auth-failures
item.description:
Verifies that the JWT auth middleware correctly guards PATCH /me/profile.
These tests do not depend on any specific user in the DB.

exec: // No Authorization header — the JWTAuth middleware must reject before handler runs.
// A JWT with the last character of the signature replaced by 'X'.
// The JWTAuth middleware validates the ECDSA signature and must reject this.

item.name: validation
item.description:
Body-size and malformed-JSON cases.
Both use prof_access_token since the JWTAuth middleware fires before body decoding.

exec: // Pad the body past the 1 MiB MaxBytesReader limit.
// http.MaxBytesReader fires during Decode → respond.DecodeJSON maps this to 413.

item.name: rate-limiting-prof
item.description:
Verifies the prof:ip: IP rate limiter for PATCH /me/profile (burst=10, rate=10/1min).

The limiter is inside the JWTAuth middleware group. Because collection variables
(including prof_access_token) are lost between separate newman invocations, all
folders including this one run in ONE invocation (see make e2e-update-profile).

This folder's prerequest overrides _xff to 127.0.0.1 so all requests here share
one prof:ip: bucket that is separate from the happy-path and failure requests
(which use unique 10.x.x.x IPs).

Warmup requests use an empty body (→ 422 at validation level) so no real DB write

exec: // Pin all requests in this folder to 127.0.0.1 so they share one prof:ip: bucket.

item.name: warmup 10/10 — consume slot (→ not 429, last allowed)

exec: pm.test('not rate-limited yet (prof:ip: slot 10 — last allowed)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.eql(429));

variable.description: Auto-incremented counter used by the collection prerequest to generate unique X-Forwarded-For IPs.
Current X-Forwarded-For value. Set to a unique IP by the collection prerequest; overridden to 127.0.0.1 by the rate-limiting-prof folder prerequest.
Set from gmail_test_email in setup/register prerequest. The verified account used throughout all folders.
Password for prof_email. Set in setup/register prerequest.
Verification OTP fetched from Gmail by setup/register test script. Consumed by setup/verify-email.
Access token obtained from setup/login. Used by all subsequent folders including rate-limiting-prof.
Tracks the most recently patched display_name so GET /me assertions can reference it.
Tracks the most recently patched avatar_url so GET /me assertions can reference it.
